C[daal: well, I've come to the conclusion that my main problem is not considering alternative movves
daal: so with a 9x9 board, I am trying to develop a positve habit
Himiko: I see. It's an interesting approach.
daal: simply to consider at least 2 moves and try to read them out
daal: I'm less concerned at the moment with the quality of my moves
C[Himiko: I think, fwiw, the main issue most players I meet have is not so much not seeing moves as not knowing what is going on in the situation at hand
daal: I just want to make sure that I've at least put some thought into each one
C[Himiko: that is, you don't need to see the very best move locally, but you need to see what is important overall - direction and what groups are strong or weak
C[daal: I'm sure you are right, but for me, I know that I can't improve without getting over the habit of not comparing scenarios
